verification_notes: Vendor contact dates set to 2026-04-12 (research date). Live HTTP verification requires network access; run `python main_research.py --verify` in a network-enabled environment to confirm vendor URL reachability.

records.tactile_description: Shackle rotation at precise angle aligns hidden notch gate with body channel. Solver must rotate shackle to exact hand angle while applying spring tension to release.
records.classification_reasoning: Sold and marketed as a puzzle; no security rating; designed for tactile exploration.

records.tactile_description: Body rotation at specific angle combined with shackle press aligns internal disk detent. Requires precise hand angle rotation to feel click detent engagement.
records.classification_reasoning: Puzzle-only product; body requires specific tilt angle for disk alignment.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1)

records.tactile_description: Two-piece assembly: shackle and body. A hidden magnetic element in the shackle must align with a magnetic detent in the body channel through precise rotation. Feel-based click signals alignment.
records.classification_reasoning: Designed exclusively as puzzle; magnetic alignment is primary solve mechanic.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); hidden magnetic element adds misdirection (+1)

records.tactile_description: Spring detent mechanism in body aligns with shackle notch via specific tilt and rotation angle. Solver must press shackle at precise angle while rotating body to feel spring detent release.
records.classification_reasoning: Marketed as a brain-teaser puzzle; no security utility; requires angle and pressure.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1)

records.tactile_description: Shackle must be pressed inward while body is tilted to specific angle; internal lever feel confirms alignment of gate notch. Both hand angle precision and inward pressure required.
records.classification_reasoning: Pure puzzle design; five in the numbered Puzzle Master trick lock series.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1)

records.tactile_description: Body and shackle form a 2-piece assembly. A recessed pressure point on the body must be depressed while rotating shackle to align hidden wafer feel mechanism.
records.classification_reasoning: Puzzle classification confirmed; sold in puzzle series, no key mechanism.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1)

records.tactile_description: Classic escape-artist novelty lock. Shackle appears locked but releases when rotated to a precise angle while applying downward pressure; alignment felt as spring detent click.
records.classification_reasoning: Named after Houdini; sold as novelty/puzzle with no security function.

records.tactile_description: Two interlocking cast metal pieces forming a lock shape. Pieces must be rotated and guided into alignment notch-by-notch using fingertip feel; no visual indicator of correct path.
records.classification_reasoning: Hanayama puzzle series; classified puzzle by manufacturer; no security use.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; pressure control needed (+1); sequential tactile steps (+2); spring detent present in notch engagement
records.notes: Hanayama rates this level 5/6; sequential notch navigation is primary challenge.

records.tactile_description: Body and shackle with hidden false gate; solver must identify correct tilt angle through feel to bypass false gate and engage true shackle release notch.
records.classification_reasoning: Traditional design reproduced as puzzle collectible; occasional security/novelty use makes it hybrid.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1)
records.notes: False gate adds misdirection; classic design dates to Qing dynasty reproduced for puzzle market.

records.tactile_description: Named after Napoleon era; two-piece brass body and shackle. Shackle rotates 90 degrees to precise angle where hidden lever feel engagement allows release. Angle precision critical.
records.classification_reasoning: Historical reproduction sold as collectible puzzle; no security rating.

records.tactile_description: Wing-shaped body rotates against shackle; both pieces must align to exact tactile click detent position simultaneously. Hand angle determines wing/shackle orientation.
records.classification_reasoning: Decorative puzzle lock; sold exclusively as puzzle novelty.

records.tactile_description: Heart-shaped body with shackle. Opening requires rotating the body relative to shackle to feel notch engagement; hand angle must be maintained to prevent false gate engagement.
records.classification_reasoning: Gifting/novelty puzzle; Creative Crafthouse markets it as a puzzle, not a security product.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); simple single false gate
records.notes: Entry-level gift puzzle; good introduction to tactile alignment concept.

records.tactile_description: Classic shackle-angle trick lock: shackle contains a hidden notch accessible only at a precise rotation angle (~37 degrees). Body spring detent clicks when correct angle achieved while downward shackle pressure applied.
records.classification_reasoning: Dedicated puzzle lock vendor product; designed and sold exclusively for puzzle use.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1)
records.notes: Precision angle ~37 degrees; non-obvious release point.

records.tactile_description: Advanced version: shackle notch at non-obvious angle (~112 degrees). False gate at obvious 90-degree position provides tactile misdirection. Solver must feel past false gate click to reach true release. Requires deliberate angle precision and pressure control.
records.classification_reasoning: Expert tier puzzle lock; same vendor as Standard; purely puzzle-oriented product.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1); hidden magnetic element adds misdirection (+1) [false gate]; sequential feel required to pass false gate (+1)
records.notes: False gate at 90 degrees actively misleads; true gate at 112 degrees. Highest tactile precision requirement in dataset.

records.tactile_description: Two-piece body with rotating inner barrel. Inner barrel must be rotated to align two internal notches via tactile feel; outer body held at fixed angle while inner piece is felt through to correct gate position.
records.classification_reasoning: Puzzle Box World puzzle category; no security rating; barrel rotation is primary solve.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); pressure control needed (+1); sequential tactile steps for two-notch alignment (+1)
records.notes: Inner-barrel style: outer body stationary, inner barrel rotated by feel.

records.tactile_description: Escape-room grade puzzle prop. Shackle contains embedded magnet; body has two magnetic detents. Solver must rotate shackle to magnetic alignment position felt as attraction pull toward correct angle. No visible indicator.
records.classification_reasoning: Designed for escape rooms as puzzle prop; magnetic alignment is primary solve mechanic.
records.difficulty_justification: Base 2; hand/angle precision required (+2); hidden magnetic element adds misdirection (+1)
records.notes: Two magnetic detents: one false (weaker pull), one release (stronger pull). Requires distinguishing pull strengths.

records.tactile_description: Vintage-style brass padlock reproduction. Two-piece body and shackle. A rotating collar on the body must be aligned with a tactile detent groove before shackle press releases. Angle of collar rotation is the puzzle element.
records.classification_reasoning: Sold as novelty/collectible puzzle; occasional use as low-security theatrical prop makes it hybrid.
records.notes: Collar rotation angle is primary solve; aesthetic design intentionally antique.

records.tactile_description: Mass-market trick lock. Shackle must be pressed and rotated simultaneously to specific hand angle; spring detent in body channel clicks when shackle notch aligns with release gate.
records.classification_reasoning: Consumer puzzle product; sold in puzzle/novelty section; no security specification.
records.notes: Widely available mass-market entry point; lower difficulty but canonical example of 2-piece shackle-angle mechanism.
